prompt: In our opendeviationbar-py project we have a simple session detection in ouroboros.py that uses zoneinfo with fixed hour ranges (e.g. tokyo start=9 end=15). It works but doesn't handle holidays, lunch breaks, or sub-hour precision (NYSE actually opens at 9:30 not 10:00). What's the upgrade path to make this production-grade? We also need it to work with our ClickHouse pipeline where we use toTimezone() for server-side session columns.

assertions.text: Recommends exchange_calendars as the upgrade target (not pandas_market_calendars or manual implementation)
Explains that ClickHouse toTimezone() handles DST but NOT holidays/lunch breaks — need Python-side computation
Provides concrete migration steps (install lib, replace dicts, replace checks, update tests)
References the existing ouroboros.py pattern and explains what it gets right vs what it misses
